text: I feel like I am in a modern, public building—perhaps an office or a museum with a focus on research and innovation. The world here is constructed and deliberate. The glass walls separate private collaboration from public exploration. I feel a little out of place, as if I'm hovering between learning and contributing. I am both observer and participant, surrounded by reminders of knowledge constantly evolving. The vivid colors and sleek design invite curiosity, yet the artificiality of it all subtly unsettles me. In this space, I wonder about the boundaries between thought and action, creation and observation.
